qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: OS Command Injection in Execute Command node allows arbitrary command execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes the Execute Command node which directly executes user-provided shell commands via child_process.exec(). The advisory describes expression evaluation vulnerabilities in workflow parameters. These are different vulnerable components: Execute Command node is designed to run commands, while expression evaluation is a separate n8n feature for evaluating template expressions like {{ $json.field }}. The vulnerable code paths and root causes do not align.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: VM2 sandbox escape in Code node allows arbitrary code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes VM2 sandbox escape in the Code node where users can execute arbitrary JavaScript. The advisory describes expression evaluation vulnerabilities in workflow parameters. The Code node and expression evaluation are separate n8n features with different execution contexts and sandbox implementations. While both achieve RCE, they use different vulnerable code paths: VM2 for Code nodes vs. the expression evaluator for workflow parameters.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery in workflow import from URL endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSRF in the /workflows/from-url endpoint. The advisory describes expression evaluation vulnerabilities leading to RCE. These are entirely different vulnerability classes (SSRF vs RCE) and different vulnerable components (URL fetch endpoint vs expression evaluator). No alignment in vulnerable code, root cause, or attack path.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory describes expression evaluation vulnerabilities in n8n's workflow parameter processing system (CVE-2026-25049, related to CVE-2025-68613). The qualifying findings describe different vulnerable components: Execute Command node (direct command execution), Code node VM2 sandbox (JavaScript execution sandbox), SSRF in workflow import endpoint, and a generic deserialization example. None of these findings address the expression evaluation system that the advisory targets.
evidence: Advisory title: 'n8n Has Expression Escape Vulnerability Leading to RCE'
Advisory summary: 'crafted expressions in workflow parameters to trigger unintended system command execution'
Advisory references patches for expression evaluation vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-68613, CVE-2026-25049)
Finding 1: Execute Command node - different component designed to execute commands
Finding 2: VM2 sandbox in Code node - different JavaScript execution environment
Finding 3: SSRF in workflow import - different vulnerability class and endpoint
